skill.name: skill-standardization
skill.description: Standardize and validate SKILL.md files against the Agent Skills specification (agentskills.io). Use when creating new skills, auditing existing skills for spec compliance, converting legacy skill formats to standard structure, or improving descriptions for reliable triggering. Triggers on: "validate skill", "create SKILL.md", "standardize skill format", "check skill spec", "skill frontmatter", "improve skill description", "add evals to skill".
skill.summary: Standardize and validate SKILL.md files against the Agent Skills specification (agentskills.io). Use...
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Security analysis confirms this is a legitimate skill standardization tool. All 153 static findings are false positives: the 'weak cryptographic algorithm' patterns incorrectly flag 'name' field validation as crypto hashes; 'shell command substitution' refers to legitimate script validation logic; 'Ruby backtick execution' flags markdown code examples; 'hardcoded URLs' are documentation links. No malicious behavior, command injection, data exfiltration, or prompt injection detected.

content.value_statement: This skill standardizes and validates SKILL.md files against the Agent Skills specification. It helps skill developers create compliant skill definitions with proper frontmatter, descriptions, and structure, ensuring reliable skill triggering and marketplace compatibility.
actual_capabilities: Validates SKILL.md frontmatter fields (name, description, allowed-tools, compatibility)
Checks name format against Agent Skills specification (lowercase, alphanumeric, hyphens)
Verifies description length and imperative trigger phrasing
Validates recommended sections (When to use, Instructions, Examples, Best practices, References)
Batch validates multiple skills in a directory
Generates detailed validation reports with errors and warnings
limitations: Does not execute skill code or test skill functionality
Does not validate scripts or reference files within the skill
Does not check for skill duplication or naming conflicts across the marketplace
Does not provide suggestions for improving skill descriptions beyond spec compliance

use_cases.title: Validate new skill before publishing
use_cases.description: Use before submitting a new skill to the marketplace to ensure it meets all Agent Skills specification requirements and will trigger reliably.

use_cases.title: Audit existing skills for compliance
use_cases.description: Batch validate all skills in a directory to identify and fix specification compliance issues across a skill library.

use_cases.title: Convert legacy skill formats
use_cases.description: Migrate older skill formats with non-standard headings and frontmatter to the current Agent Skills specification.

prompt_templates.title: Validate a single skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate my SKILL.md file at .agent-skills/my-new-skill/SKILL.md against the Agent Skills specification. Check all required fields, name format, description length, and recommended sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking a newly created skill for specification compliance

prompt_templates.title: Create a new SKILL.md
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new SKILL.md for a skill that handles PDF operations including extraction, form filling, and merging. The skill should trigger when users need to work with PDF files.
prompt_templates.scenario: Generating a properly formatted skill definition

prompt_templates.title: Improve skill description
prompt_templates.prompt: My skill description is 'Helps with CSV files.' Improve it so the skill triggers more reliably on relevant prompts, following Agent Skills best practices.

prompt_templates.title: Batch validate skill directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate all skills in the .agent-skills directory and provide a summary showing which skills have errors, which have warnings, and which are fully compliant.

output_examples.output:
Validating: ./my-skill/SKILL.md
✓ Required field: name = 'my-skill'
✓ Required field: description present
✓ Description length: 256 chars (OK)
✓ Name format: valid (lowercase alphanumeric + hyphens)
✓ Name matches directory: 'my-skill'
✓ Recommended section: When to use this skill
✓ Recommended section: Instructions
✓ Recommended section: Examples
✓ File length: 180 lines (OK)

Issues: 0 errors, 0 warnings

output_examples.input: Create SKILL.md for a git workflow skill
output_examples.output:
---
name: git-workflow
description: >
  Manage git workflows including committing changes, creating branches, and handling pull requests.
  Use when the user needs to work with git repositories, even if they do not say 'git' explicitly.
  Triggers on: commit changes, create branch, pull request, merge branches, git status.
allowed-tools: Bash Read Write Edit Glob Grep
metadata:
  tags: git, workflow, version-control
  version: "1.0"
---

# Git Workflow

## When to use this skill
- Creating commits with appropriate messages
- Managing branches (create, switch, delete)
- Handling pull requests

best_practices: Focus on description quality first - weak descriptions prevent skill activation
Use imperative trigger phrasing like 'Use when...' to improve triggering reliability
Add 2-5 evaluation test cases (evals/evals.json) before publishing to marketplace
anti_patterns: Using vague descriptions like 'Helps with files' that never trigger
Omitting the frontmatter block or placing it after content
Using YAML list syntax for allowed-tools instead of space-delimited strings

faq.question: What is the Agent Skills specification?
faq.answer: The Agent Skills specification (agentskills.io) defines the standard format for skill definitions in Claude Code and compatible agents. It includes frontmatter fields, directory structure, and documentation sections.

faq.question: Why does my skill description need imperative phrasing?
faq.answer: The description determines when your skill triggers. Imperative phrasing like 'Use when...' helps the AI reliably match user intents to skills. Weak descriptions cause skills to never activate.

faq.question: What is the maximum description length?
faq.answer: The description field must be between 1 and 1024 characters. Keep it concise but include trigger keywords and synonyms to improve activation reliability.

faq.question: Can I validate multiple skills at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Run 'bash scripts/validate_skill.sh --all .agent-skills/' to batch validate all skills in a directory. The script reports errors and warnings per skill with a summary.

faq.question: What are evals and should I add them?
faq.answer: Evals are evaluation test cases in evals/evals.json that verify your skill triggers correctly. Adding 2-5 test cases is recommended before publishing to ensure reliable behavior.

faq.question: What tools can my skill use?
faq.answer: Skills can declare allowed tools in frontmatter using the allowed-tools field. Common tools include Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, and Grep. Only pre-approved tools work with Claude Code.
